message:
chore: complete audit batch 491 — 7 files deep diff-audited

scope.rs: block_on deadlock fix, winner panic precedence
h2/frame.rs: frame_length clamp->assert
watch.rs: poll_changed extraction, eager waiter cleanup
postgres.rs: SCRAM DoS cap, Box TLS, flush write_all
sqlite.rs: eager rollback spawn in transaction Drop
circuit_breaker.rs: threshold clamping
mutex.rs: wake outside lock, ManuallyDrop simplification

All SOUND. Close bead 8tdq19.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-8nc55","title":"Phase 4: Distributed Trace (Causal Ordering + Convergent State)","description":"# Phase 4: Distributed Trace (Causal Ordering + Convergent State)

## Purpose
Make distributed traces honest:
- represent time as a partial order (causal ordering), not a fake total order
- ensure obligation/lease state converges across replicas via join-semilattice rules

This is required for replay/debugging and for making distributed structured concurrency coherent.

## Core Elements
- Vector clocks (or equivalent causal metadata)
- Trace events that include node identity and causal metadata
- Obligation/lease state lattice with explicit conflict states

## Acceptance Criteria
- Trace metadata supports causal ordering (vector clocks or equivalent) so concurrent remote events remain unordered.
- Obligation/lease state converges via a join-semilattice (CRDT-style) with explicit conflict detection.
- Lab simulation can replay distributed traces deterministically up to the causal model.

 {"id":"asupersync-8rv2c","title":"[AA][Perf] Isomorphism proof template + policy","description":"## Goal
Create an isomorphism proof template for every performance change (behavior unchanged).

## Why
Performance gains are useless if semantics change. We need a standardized proof of equivalence.

## Deliverables
- Template describing ordering/tie‑breaks/RNG/floating behavior.
- Required golden outputs check procedure.
- Documentation + examples.

## Acceptance
- Every perf PR uses the template.
- CI fails if proof + checksums are missing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:05.149928930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:47:56.358836338Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:47:56.358759926Z","close_reason":"Added isomorphism proof template + policy to docs/benchmarking.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8rv2c","depends_on_id":"asupersync-43clv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8rv2c","depends_on_id":"asupersync-aaj26","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-8vb12","title":"Phase 0: Records, State Machines, and Global Σ","description":"# Phase 0: Records, State Machines, and Global Σ

## Purpose
Implement the runtime’s internal state as an explicit machine state (Σ) that matches the operational semantics.

Everything in Phase 0 should be explainable as transitions over:
- regions (R)
- tasks (T)
- obligations (O)
- time (now)

This feature covers the *data model* and *state machines* that make the kernel enforce structured concurrency, cancellation, and linear obligations.

## Scope
### 1) State enums (the protocol surface)
- `TaskState`: `Created | Running | CancelRequested | Cancelling | Finalizing | Completed(outcome)`
- `RegionState`: `Open | Closing | Draining | Finalizing | Closed(outcome)`
- `ObligationState`: `Reserved | Committed | Aborted | Leaked` (terminal states are absorbing)
- `ObligationKind`: at minimum `SendPermit | Ack | Lease | IoOp` (even if Lease/IoOp are Phase 2+/4+, the kind set is part of the semantic model)

### 2) Records (the owned runtime resources)
- `TaskRecord`: owned by exactly one region; maintains waiters; tracks mask deferrals.
- `RegionRecord`: parent/children/subregions; finalizer stack; policy; effective budget; cancel reason.
- `ObligationRecord` + `ObligationRegistry`: ties linear obligations to holder task + owning region; enforces leak detection.

### 3) Global runtime state Σ
- Container that holds all arenas/registries and current time.
- Provides invariants checks for lab/debug.

## Non-Negotiable Invariants (must be representable and checkable)
- Ownership tree (regions form a rooted tree)
- All live tasks are owned by a region
- Region close implies quiescence
- Cancel propagates down the tree
- Reserved obligations have live holders
- Obligations are linear (resolve at most once)
- Mask deferral is bounded and monotone

## Acceptance Criteria
- There is an explicit data model that supports all transition rules:
  - spawn/schedule/complete
  - cancel request/acknowledge/drain/finalize
  - reserve/commit/abort/leak
  - join waiting
  - region close phases
  - tick/timeouts
- The model is compatible with deterministic testing: invariants can be checked from state snapshots and/or traces.

## Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for each enum transition validity.
- Unit tests for registries/arenas.
- Property tests for “no illegal transitions” under randomized sequences (lab deterministic).
